title: St. John The Baptist Jacobite Syrian Church, South Parur
tagline: St. John The Baptist Jacobite Syrian Church, South Parur — spiritual town of Kerala

heroImage.alt: St. John The Baptist Jacobite Syrian Church, South Parur, Kerala

overview.short: St. John The Baptist Jacobite Syrian Church, South Parur is a much-loved spiritual town in Kerala, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. John The Baptist is a Syrian Catholic church situated in South Parur, a village located on southern border of Ernakulam District, Kerala, India. This area is landmarked by the busy Kottayam-Ernakulam Road (Vaikom Road).

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: St. Sebastian's Church, Udayamperoor
topPlaces.description: St. Sebastian's Church, Udayamperoor, is a Christian church in Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Amballur, Ernakulam
topPlaces.description: Amballur is a suburban region of Kochi in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Perumbalam
topPlaces.description: Perumbalam is an island village in the Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. As of 2001 India census, Perumbalam had a population of 9,733 with 4,880 males and 4,853 females.

topPlaces.name: Kanjiramattom
topPlaces.description: Kanjiramattom in Ernakulam District, Kerala, India is a suburban area of the City of Kochi lying approximately 25 km southeast to the City of Kochi. The place is known for the Arayankavu Devi Temple And Sheikh Fariddudin Mosque and its proximity to the famous Arayankavu Temple.

topPlaces.name: Kerala backwaters
topPlaces.description: The Kerala backwaters are a network of brackish lagoons and canals running parallel to the Arabian Sea along the Malabar coast of Kerala state in south-western India. It also includes interconnected lakes, rivers, and inlets, a labyrinthine system formed by more than 900 km (560 mi) of waterways, and sometimes compared to bayous.

topPlaces.name: Perumpilly
topPlaces.description: Perumpilly, also Perumpally, is a village in Kanayannur taluk of Ernakulam district in Kerala state, south India. It is 22 km (14 mi) south-east of Ernakulam city.

topPlaces.name: Maradu
topPlaces.description: Maradu (Malayalam pronunciation: [mɐɾɐɖɨ̆]) is a municipality and census town in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India. Maradu is an inner suburb of the Kochi metropolitan area and is located 6 km (3.7 mi) south of the Kochi city centre.

topPlaces.name: Vembanad
topPlaces.description: Vembanad (Malayalam: [ʋeːmbɐnaːɖə̆]) is the longest lake in India, and the largest in the state of Kerala. The lake has an area of 2,033 square kilometres (785 sq mi) and a maximum length of 96.5 km (60 mi).

Amballur is a suburban region of Kochi in the Indian state of Kerala.
Perumbalam is an island village in the Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala. As of 2001 India census, Perumbalam had a population of 9,733 with 4,880 males and 4,853 females.

Kanjiramattom in Ernakulam District, Kerala, India is a suburban area of the City of Kochi lying approximately 25 km southeast to the City of Kochi. The place is known for the Arayankavu Devi Temple And Sheikh Fariddudin Mosque and its proximity to the famous Arayankavu Temple.

The Kerala backwaters are a network of brackish lagoons and canals running parallel to the Arabian Sea along the Malabar coast of Kerala state in south-western India. It also includes interconnected lakes, rivers, and inlets, a labyrinthine system formed by more than 900 km (560 mi) of waterways, and sometimes compared to bayous.
Perumpilly, also Perumpally, is a village in Kanayannur taluk of Ernakulam district in Kerala state, south India. It is 22 km (14 mi) south-east of Ernakulam city.

Maradu (Malayalam pronunciation: [mɐɾɐɖɨ̆]) is a municipality and census town in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India. Maradu is an inner suburb of the Kochi metropolitan area and is located 6 km (3.7 mi) south of the Kochi city centre.

Vembanad (Malayalam: [ʋeːmbɐnaːɖə̆]) is the longest lake in India, and the largest in the state of Kerala. The lake has an area of 2,033 square kilometres (785 sq mi) and a maximum length of 96.5 km (60 mi).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit St. John The Baptist Jacobite Syrian Church, South Parur?
faq.a: The best time to visit St. John The Baptist Jacobite Syrian Church, South Parur is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach St. John The Baptist Jacobite Syrian Church, South Parur?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (32 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (16 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for St. John The Baptist Jacobite Syrian Church, South Parur?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of St. John The Baptist Jacobite Syrian Church, South Parur at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in St. John The Baptist Jacobite Syrian Church, South Parur?
faq.a: Stays in St. John The Baptist Jacobite Syrian Church, South Parur start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in St. John The Baptist Jacobite Syrian Church, South Parur?
faq.a: The top attractions are St. Sebastian's Church, Udayamperoor, Amballur, Ernakulam, Perumbalam, Kanjiramattom, Kerala backwaters.
